string_table.name: npnxnanbnsnfns10111100111
string_table.string: Use the w, a, s, and d keys to move around the 360 degree image. Press the tab key to jump to interactive markers and hotspots.
To watch this video, use the preview feature in Storyline 360, upload your published output to a server, publish to Review 360, or switch to static video quality.
Streaming video is unavailable for local playback.
You must complete the question before submitting.
Enter your name in the field below:
You have reached the time limit set for the quiz. Press 'OK' to continue.

slidelink.displaytext: What should you do in this course
Will you help me travel around the world?
You are ready for the adventure!
Activity: Name the stages of water cycle
Activity: Pick a source of water vapour
Where to go after it rains
Help the farmer water the plant
Review why is the water cycle important
Is it rain formation or the water cycle
1. What is the process by which rain is formed?
2. What happens to rainwater after it falls to the ground, completing the water cycle?
3. What happens during the process of evaporation in the water cycle?
4. What is condensation in the water cycle?
5. What is the correct sequence of stages in the water cycle?
6. Which of the following are sources of water vapour in the water cycle? Please select all options that apply.
7. What is the primary reason the water cycle is considered a continuous process?
8. What role does rainwater play in supporting plants, animals, and ecosystems?
9. Why is the water cycle important for sustaining life on Earth?
10. Which statement correctly differentiates rain formation from the water cycle?

search.slidetext: ooops! please type your name first before clicking log in button ! ok type your full name here i can’t wait to learn with you! log in back next
back jegede susan oluwatoyin matric no: 253526 department of science and technology education, faculty of education, university of ibadan. +2348061242134 susanjeg@gmail.com meet the developer next
%textentry% back next

up here in the clouds, water comes together and gets heavy. continue when the clouds are full, i fall down as rain! continue rainwater flows into rivers, lakes, and oceans, where my journey begins again. continue the sun is very hot!it helps water change into vapour and rise into the sky. continue back
learning objectives by the end of this lesson, you will be able to: back next
study each of the button below to know their function home play exit/close this lesson! back menu back next

oops! not quite. let’s look again and try another place. you can do it!” incorrect continue back next yay! you did it! that’s the right place for me. great job helping me, superstar! correct continue back next click the location where i belong note: only select one option that is right for me water bank of water grass road desert desert back

oops! that’s not quite right. think again and try once more. remember, learning is fun when you click and listen! incorrect continue excellent work!you are ready to learn with me. let’s continue our journey! correct continue b skip everything c close the lesson a click and listen. back next
back next
back next %textentry%

complete all the lesson and quiz before you can access certificate for this course. warning ok study each of the button below to know their function home next play exit/close back lesson menu back next
ooops! please type your full name first before clicking on continue button ! ok your full name back next

incorrect that is incorrect. please try again. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect you did not select the correct response. continue continue continue continue continue continue correct that's right! you selected the correct response. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next evaporation precipitation condensation collection back menu

lakes release water vapour into the air water vapor comes from lakes through a natural process called evaporation, where liquid water changes into an invisible gas and rises into the atmosphere. back next which living thing releases water vapour? which living thing releases water vapour out into the air back next back next menu which living thing releases water vapour? back next

x back next rain formation is only one step, when water falls from the clouds to earth. the water cycle includes all the steps water goes through. x back next rain formation rain formation rain formation water cycle water cycle water cycle back menu
back menu
back menu
back menu

incorrect this answer combines correct and incorrect elements, making it a plausible but incorrect choice. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this answer introduces a common misconception about how rain forms, which helps learners differentiate between correct and incorrect processes. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next correct this answer accurately describes the process of rain formation, aligning with the learning objective. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this answer includes irrelevant but plausible information, helping learners focus on the actual process of rain formation. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next the sun heats water, turning it into vapor, which rises, forms clouds, and falls as rain. the sun heats water, turning it into vapor, which rises, forms clouds, and falls as rain. the sun heats water, turning it into vapor, which rises, forms clouds, and falls as rain. the sun heats water, turning it into vapor, which rises, forms clouds, and falls as rain. the sun heats water, turning it into vapor, which rises, forms clouds, and falls as rain. the sun heats water, turning it into vapor, which rises, forms clouds, and falls as rain. rain forms when clouds are blown by the wind and collide with each other. rain forms when clouds are blown by the wind and collide with each other. rain forms when clouds are blown by the wind and collide with each other. rain forms when clouds are blown by the wind and collide with each other. rain forms when clouds are blown by the wind and collide with each other. rain forms when clouds are blown by the wind and collide with each other. rain forms when the ground becomes too wet and releases water into the air. rain forms when the ground becomes too wet and releases water into the air. rain forms when the ground becomes too wet and releases water into the air. rain forms when the ground becomes too wet and releases water into the air. rain forms when the ground becomes too wet and releases water into the air. rain forms when the ground becomes too wet and releases water into the air. rain forms when water from rivers and lakes directly falls back to the ground. rain forms when water from rivers and lakes directly falls back to the ground. rain forms when water from rivers and lakes directly falls back to the ground. rain forms when water from rivers and lakes directly falls back to the ground. rain forms when water from rivers and lakes directly falls back to the ground. rain forms when water from rivers and lakes directly falls back to the ground. 1. what is the process by which rain is formed?

incorrect this is incorrect because while evaporation is part of the water cycle, it does not occur immediately after rainwater reaches the ground. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next correct this is the correct answer because it accurately describes the process of rainwater moving into larger bodies of water, completing the water cycle. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this is incorrect because water does not remain permanently on the ground; it continues to move through the water cycle. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this is incorrect because rainwater does not freeze immediately unless specific conditions are present, which are not part of the general water cycle. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next it stays on the ground permanently. it stays on the ground permanently. it stays on the ground permanently. it stays on the ground permanently. it stays on the ground permanently. it stays on the ground permanently. it flows into rivers, lakes, and oceans. it flows into rivers, lakes, and oceans. it flows into rivers, lakes, and oceans. it flows into rivers, lakes, and oceans. it flows into rivers, lakes, and oceans. it flows into rivers, lakes, and oceans. it evaporates immediately after hitting the ground. it evaporates immediately after hitting the ground. it evaporates immediately after hitting the ground. it evaporates immediately after hitting the ground. it evaporates immediately after hitting the ground. it evaporates immediately after hitting the ground. it turns into ice immediately after falling. it turns into ice immediately after falling. it turns into ice immediately after falling. it turns into ice immediately after falling. it turns into ice immediately after falling. it turns into ice immediately after falling. 2. what happens to rainwater after it falls to the ground, completing the water cycle?

incorrect this describes freezing, not evaporation, and does not involve the sun's heat. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this describes transpiration, not evaporation, and involves plants rather than the sun's heat. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next correct this is the correct description of evaporation as it aligns with the scientific process where water changes state due to heat. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this describes runoff, not evaporation, and does not involve a change in state due to heat. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next water is heated by the sun and turns into water vapour. water is heated by the sun and turns into water vapour. water is heated by the sun and turns into water vapour. water is heated by the sun and turns into water vapour. water is heated by the sun and turns into water vapour. water is heated by the sun and turns into water vapour. water is absorbed by plants and used for growth. water is absorbed by plants and used for growth. water is absorbed by plants and used for growth. water is absorbed by plants and used for growth. water is absorbed by plants and used for growth. water is absorbed by plants and used for growth. water cools down and turns into ice. water cools down and turns into ice. water cools down and turns into ice. water cools down and turns into ice. water cools down and turns into ice. water cools down and turns into ice. 3. what happens during the process of evaporation in the water cycle? water flows downhill into rivers and lakes. water flows downhill into rivers and lakes. water flows downhill into rivers and lakes. water flows downhill into rivers and lakes. water flows downhill into rivers and lakes. water flows downhill into rivers and lakes.

incorrect this answer describes precipitation, not condensation, and therefore does not correctly address the question. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this answer describes evaporation, not condensation, and is therefore incorrect. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this answer describes collection, not condensation, and is therefore incorrect. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next correct this answer is correct because it accurately describes the process of condensation as it occurs in the water cycle. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next the process where water flows over the ground and collects in rivers, lakes, or oceans. the process where water flows over the ground and collects in rivers, lakes, or oceans. the process where water flows over the ground and collects in rivers, lakes, or oceans. the process where water flows over the ground and collects in rivers, lakes, or oceans. the process where water flows over the ground and collects in rivers, lakes, or oceans. the process where water flows over the ground and collects in rivers, lakes, or oceans. the process where the sun heats water and turns it into water vapour. the process where the sun heats water and turns it into water vapour. the process where the sun heats water and turns it into water vapour. the process where the sun heats water and turns it into water vapour. the process where the sun heats water and turns it into water vapour. the process where the sun heats water and turns it into water vapour. the process where water droplets combine to form larger drops and fall to the ground. the process where water droplets combine to form larger drops and fall to the ground. the process where water droplets combine to form larger drops and fall to the ground. the process where water droplets combine to form larger drops and fall to the ground. the process where water droplets combine to form larger drops and fall to the ground. the process where water droplets combine to form larger drops and fall to the ground. 4. what is condensation in the water cycle? the process where water vapour cools and changes into tiny water droplets, forming clouds. the process where water vapour cools and changes into tiny water droplets, forming clouds. the process where water vapour cools and changes into tiny water droplets, forming clouds. the process where water vapour cools and changes into tiny water droplets, forming clouds. the process where water vapour cools and changes into tiny water droplets, forming clouds. the process where water vapour cools and changes into tiny water droplets, forming clouds. click to add title

correct this is the correct sequence of the water cycle stages, reflecting the natural process of water movement on earth. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this sequence is incorrect because it places collection before evaporation, which is not the correct order of the water cycle. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this sequence is incorrect because it starts with precipitation, which does not initiate the water cycle. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this sequence is incorrect because it places condensation before evaporation, which disrupts the natural flow of the water cycle. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next precipitation, collection, evaporation, condensation precipitation, collection, evaporation, condensation precipitation, collection, evaporation, condensation precipitation, collection, evaporation, condensation precipitation, collection, evaporation, condensation precipitation, collection, evaporation, condensation collection, evaporation, precipitation, condensation collection, evaporation, precipitation, condensation collection, evaporation, precipitation, condensation collection, evaporation, precipitation, condensation collection, evaporation, precipitation, condensation collection, evaporation, precipitation, condensation evaporation, condensation, precipitation, collection evaporation, condensation, precipitation, collection evaporation, condensation, precipitation, collection evaporation, condensation, precipitation, collection evaporation, condensation, precipitation, collection evaporation, condensation, precipitation, collection 5. what is the correct sequence of stages in the water cycle? condensation, precipitation, evaporation, collection condensation, precipitation, evaporation, collection condensation, precipitation, evaporation, collection condensation, precipitation, evaporation, collection condensation, precipitation, evaporation, collection condensation, precipitation, evaporation, collection

incorrect that is incorrect. please try again. try again try again try again try again try again try again incorrect mountains do not directly release water vapour into the air; they may influence weather patterns but are not a source of evaporation or transpiration. sand dunes do not release water vapour as they lack significant water content for evaporation. clouds are formed by condensation of water vapour but are not a source of water vapour themselves. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next correct plants release water vapour into the air through a process called transpiration. water from rivers and lakes evaporates into the air as water vapour. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next sand dunes sand dunes sand dunes sand dunes sand dunes sand dunes clouds clouds clouds clouds clouds clouds plants through transpiration plants through transpiration plants through transpiration plants through transpiration plants through transpiration plants through transpiration mountains mountains mountains mountains mountains mountains 6. which of the following are sources of water vapour in the water cycle? please select all options that apply. rivers and lakes rivers and lakes rivers and lakes rivers and lakes rivers and lakes rivers and lakes

incorrect this answer is incorrect because the water cycle continues through collection and evaporation after precipitation. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this answer is incorrect because the water cycle involves changes in state, such as evaporation and condensation, not just movement between water bodies. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next correct this answer is correct because it accurately describes the continuous nature of the water cycle and its stages. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this answer is incorrect because water does not leave earth's atmosphere permanently; it remains within the water cycle. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next water moves through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection in a never-ending cycle. water moves through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection in a never-ending cycle. water moves through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection in a never-ending cycle. water moves through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection in a never-ending cycle. water moves through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection in a never-ending cycle. water moves through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection in a never-ending cycle. water only moves between rivers and oceans without changing states. water only moves between rivers and oceans without changing states. water only moves between rivers and oceans without changing states. water only moves between rivers and oceans without changing states. water only moves between rivers and oceans without changing states. water only moves between rivers and oceans without changing states. the water cycle stops after precipitation occurs. the water cycle stops after precipitation occurs. the water cycle stops after precipitation occurs. the water cycle stops after precipitation occurs. the water cycle stops after precipitation occurs. the water cycle stops after precipitation occurs. 7. what is the primary reason the water cycle is considered a continuous process? water evaporates into space and does not return to earth. water evaporates into space and does not return to earth. water evaporates into space and does not return to earth. water evaporates into space and does not return to earth. water evaporates into space and does not return to earth. water evaporates into space and does not return to earth.

incorrect this answer is incorrect because rainwater does not remain on roads and pavements as a primary source for ecosystems. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next correct this answer is correct because it accurately describes the role of rainwater in supporting ecosystems, aligning with the learning objective. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this answer is incorrect because it ignores the role of rainwater in soil and rivers, which are crucial for plants and animals. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this answer is incorrect because it contradicts the role of rainwater in supporting ecosystems by suggesting it has no impact. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next rainwater only collects in oceans and does not interact with plants or animals. rainwater only collects in oceans and does not interact with plants or animals. rainwater only collects in oceans and does not interact with plants or animals. rainwater only collects in oceans and does not interact with plants or animals. rainwater only collects in oceans and does not interact with plants or animals. rainwater only collects in oceans and does not interact with plants or animals. rainwater soaks into the soil to help plants grow, flows into rivers to support aquatic life, and provides fresh water for animals and humans. rainwater soaks into the soil to help plants grow, flows into rivers to support aquatic life, and provides fresh water for animals and humans. rainwater soaks into the soil to help plants grow, flows into rivers to support aquatic life, and provides fresh water for animals and humans. rainwater soaks into the soil to help plants grow, flows into rivers to support aquatic life, and provides fresh water for animals and humans. rainwater soaks into the soil to help plants grow, flows into rivers to support aquatic life, and provides fresh water for animals and humans. rainwater soaks into the soil to help plants grow, flows into rivers to support aquatic life, and provides fresh water for animals and humans. rainwater stays on roads and pavements, providing water for urban plants and animals. rainwater stays on roads and pavements, providing water for urban plants and animals. rainwater stays on roads and pavements, providing water for urban plants and animals. rainwater stays on roads and pavements, providing water for urban plants and animals. rainwater stays on roads and pavements, providing water for urban plants and animals. rainwater stays on roads and pavements, providing water for urban plants and animals. 8. what role does rainwater play in supporting plants, animals, and ecosystems? rainwater evaporates immediately after falling to the ground, leaving no impact on ecosystems. rainwater evaporates immediately after falling to the ground, leaving no impact on ecosystems. rainwater evaporates immediately after falling to the ground, leaving no impact on ecosystems. rainwater evaporates immediately after falling to the ground, leaving no impact on ecosystems. rainwater evaporates immediately after falling to the ground, leaving no impact on ecosystems. rainwater evaporates immediately after falling to the ground, leaving no impact on ecosystems.

incorrect this answer is incorrect because the water cycle impacts all living organisms, not just aquatic animals. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next correct this answer is correct because the water cycle ensures the availability of water, which is essential for all forms of life on earth. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this answer is incorrect because the water cycle supports all life forms, not just plants. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this answer is incorrect because, while the water cycle influences weather, it also plays a critical role in sustaining life. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next it only helps plants grow but does not affect animals or humans. it only helps plants grow but does not affect animals or humans. it only helps plants grow but does not affect animals or humans. it only helps plants grow but does not affect animals or humans. it only helps plants grow but does not affect animals or humans. it only helps plants grow but does not affect animals or humans. it provides water necessary for plants, animals, and humans to survive. it provides water necessary for plants, animals, and humans to survive. it provides water necessary for plants, animals, and humans to survive. it provides water necessary for plants, animals, and humans to survive. it provides water necessary for plants, animals, and humans to survive. it provides water necessary for plants, animals, and humans to survive. it only affects aquatic animals living in rivers, lakes, and oceans. it only affects aquatic animals living in rivers, lakes, and oceans. it only affects aquatic animals living in rivers, lakes, and oceans. it only affects aquatic animals living in rivers, lakes, and oceans. it only affects aquatic animals living in rivers, lakes, and oceans. it only affects aquatic animals living in rivers, lakes, and oceans. 9. why is the water cycle important for sustaining life on earth? it creates weather patterns but has no role in sustaining life. it creates weather patterns but has no role in sustaining life. it creates weather patterns but has no role in sustaining life. it creates weather patterns but has no role in sustaining life. it creates weather patterns but has no role in sustaining life. it creates weather patterns but has no role in sustaining life.

incorrect this answer is incorrect because it misrepresents the continuous nature of the water cycle and incorrectly attributes continuity to rain formation. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next correct this answer is correct because it accurately describes the relationship between rain formation and the water cycle. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this answer is incorrect because it reverses the processes involved in rain formation and the water cycle, leading to a misunderstanding of their components. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next incorrect this answer is incorrect because it conflates rain formation with the entire water cycle, failing to recognize that rain formation is just one step. continue continue continue continue continue continue back next rain formation involves evaporation and condensation, while the water cycle only includes precipitation. rain formation involves evaporation and condensation, while the water cycle only includes precipitation. rain formation involves evaporation and condensation, while the water cycle only includes precipitation. rain formation involves evaporation and condensation, while the water cycle only includes precipitation. rain formation involves evaporation and condensation, while the water cycle only includes precipitation. rain formation involves evaporation and condensation, while the water cycle only includes precipitation. rain formation is one step, while the water cycle includes all steps like evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection. rain formation is one step, while the water cycle includes all steps like evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection. rain formation is one step, while the water cycle includes all steps like evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection. rain formation is one step, while the water cycle includes all steps like evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection. rain formation is one step, while the water cycle includes all steps like evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection. rain formation is one step, while the water cycle includes all steps like evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection. rain formation happens continuously, while the water cycle occurs only during rainy seasons. rain formation happens continuously, while the water cycle occurs only during rainy seasons. rain formation happens continuously, while the water cycle occurs only during rainy seasons. rain formation happens continuously, while the water cycle occurs only during rainy seasons. rain formation happens continuously, while the water cycle occurs only during rainy seasons. rain formation happens continuously, while the water cycle occurs only during rainy seasons. 10. which statement correctly differentiates rain formation from the water cycle? rain formation and the water cycle are the same process. rain formation and the water cycle are the same process. rain formation and the water cycle are the same process. rain formation and the water cycle are the same process. rain formation and the water cycle are the same process. rain formation and the water cycle are the same process. click to add title

resource_data.description: Here are some useful links and documents:
